name: blockrun
description: Use when user needs capabilities Claude lacks (image generation, real-time X/Twitter data) or explicitly requests external models ("blockrun", "use grok", "use gpt", "dall-e", "deepseek")
capabilities: Route requests to external LLMs (GPT, Grok, Gemini, DeepSeek) via x402 micropayments
Generate images using DALL-E or Nano Banana models
Access real-time X/Twitter data via xAI Live Search integration
Track per-call spending with budget limits and balance checks
Support multiple AI providers without managing individual API keys
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 64 patterns that are false positives: markdown code block delimiters misidentified as shell backticks (58), hidden directory path in documentation (4), and no actual crypto code present (2). The skill is documentation for a legitimate cross-model AI SDK using wallet-based micropayments. External command and filesystem risks are from documented Python SDK usage patterns, not executable malicious code.
valueStatement: Access capabilities your AI lacks like image generation and real-time X data. Pay per token with crypto wallet instead of managing API keys.
userTitle: Route AI tasks to external models with crypto micropayments
limitations: Requires crypto wallet funding with USDC on Base network
Per-token costs accumulate and require active budget monitoring
Real-time X data requires explicit search parameters to activate
Cannot execute external models without user wallet authorization

useCases.targetUser: Software developers using AI coding assistants
useCases.title: AI Developer needing second opinions
useCases.description: Route code reviews or complex reasoning tasks to GPT-5.2 while working primarily in Claude Code. Get alternative perspectives on architecture decisions.

useCases.targetUser: Marketing professionals and social media managers
useCases.title: Social media manager requiring live data
useCases.description: Access real-time X/Twitter posts and trending topics via Grok Live Search when your primary AI lacks live internet access.

useCases.title: Content creator needing image generation
useCases.description: Generate images for blog posts or social media directly within your AI workflow using DALL-E integration without switching tools.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Image Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: blockrun generate an image of [describe your image]. Use DALL-E for photorealistic results.

promptTemplates.title: Real-time X Data Lookup
promptTemplates.prompt: use grok to check what [handle or topic] is posting on X right now. Enable live search.

promptTemplates.title: Second Opinion on Code
promptTemplates.prompt: blockrun second opinion with GPT on this code: [paste code]. Review for bugs and suggest improvements.
promptTemplates.scenario: Code review with alternative AI perspective

promptTemplates.title: Budget-Controlled Multi-Model Task
promptTemplates.prompt: Use blockrun with max $2 budget. First check my balance, then use Grok with live search to research [topic], summarize findings with GPT-5.2, and generate a cover image with DALL-E. Report total spending.
promptTemplates.scenario: Complex multi-step task with spending control

bestPractices: Set explicit budget limits before starting multi-step tasks to prevent unexpected charges
Use max_search_results parameter with Grok Live Search to control per-query costs
Check wallet balance before large operations and monitor spending after each call
Choose cost-appropriate models: DeepSeek for simple tasks, GPT-5.2 for complex reasoning
antiPatterns: Making repeated Live Search calls without setting max_search_results - costs accumulate quickly at $0.025 per source
Using expensive models like GPT-5.2 for simple tasks that DeepSeek could handle at fraction of cost
Forgetting to report spending to users after completing paid operations - always show transaction summary
Calling external models for tasks Claude can handle natively - adds unnecessary cost and latency

faq.question: How do I fund my BlockRun wallet?
faq.answer: Run setup_agent_wallet() to generate your wallet address and QR code. Send USDC on Base network to the displayed address. Minimum recommended: $1-5 for testing.

faq.question: What happens if I exceed my budget?
faq.answer: BlockRun tracks spending per session. When budget is reached, it stops making calls and notifies you. You can set a new budget or continue manually.

faq.question: Do I need separate API keys for each model provider?
faq.answer: No. BlockRun uses x402 micropayments from your wallet to pay providers directly. One wallet works across OpenAI, xAI, Google, and DeepSeek.

faq.question: How much does a typical operation cost?
faq.answer: A 500-word GPT-5.2 prompt costs ~$0.001 input. A DALL-E image costs $0.04. Grok Live Search costs $0.025 per source (default 10 sources = $0.25).

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. BlockRun is designed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It extends capabilities these AIs lack like image generation and real-time X data.

faq.question: Where is my wallet data stored?
faq.answer: Wallet credentials are stored in $HOME/.blockrun/.session on your local machine. This is a standard hidden directory for CLI tool configuration.
